channel.title: Code Defence Intelligence Brief — Thu‚ 19 Mar 2026
channel.description: Daily cybersecurity intelligence for Thu‚ 19 Mar 2026.

item.title: Interlock ransomware exploited Cisco FMC zero-day for 36 days before disclosure. 🛡️
item.description:
The Interlock ransomware group had a one-month head start on your defense team. 🛡️
CVE-2026-20131 · Severity 10.0 · Critical Java Deserialization in Cisco Secure FMC.
Amazon Threat Intelligence has confirmed that the Interlock ransomware group has been weaponizing a critical flaw in @[Cisco]\\(urn:li:organization:1063\\) Secure Firewall Management Center ❨FMC❩ since January 26‚ 2026. This unauthenticated RCE allows attackers to bypass authentication and execute Java code as root.
The vulnerability stems from insecure deserialization of user-supplied Java byte streams. Because FMC manages your entire firewall estate‚ root access here allows an attacker to dismantle network security controls and deploy ransomware globally from a single trusted node.
The uncomfortable truth: Your perimeter security tools are now the primary vehicles for automated ransomware delivery because they often operate with the highest privileges and the least oversight.
→ Update Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center to the latest patched version immediately. → Strictly isolate all management plane interfaces from the public internet using a secure VPN or OOB management network. → Audit your FMC logs for unauthorized administrative accounts or anomalous Java process executions dating back to January.
#Cybersecurity #Ransomware #Cisco #ZeroDay #PatchManagement #CodeDefence

item.title: DarkSword exploit kit targets millions of iPhones for full device takeover. 📱
item.description:
A new Russian-linked exploit kit is silently compromising iPhones via infected websites. 📱
DarkSword · Targets iOS 18.4 to 18.7 · Full Device Takeover and Data Theft.
Joint research from @[Google]\\(urn:li:organization:1441\\)‚ iVerify‚ and Lookout has identified a sophisticated new spyware delivery framework dubbed DarkSword. Hosted on compromised government servers in Ukraine‚ the kit uses six distinct flaws to gain root control over iPhones when a user simply visits a malicious site.
The spyware exfiltrates Wi-Fi passwords‚ call history‚ root location history‚ and specifically targets cryptocurrency wallets and health databases. Researchers estimate that up to 270 million iPhones remain vulnerable due to running outdated software released in mid-2025.
The uncomfortable truth: Your mobile devices are no longer just communication tools; they are the primary targets for high-speed‚ automated espionage and financial theft.
→ Force a security update for all managed @[Apple]\\(urn:li:organization:162479\\) devices to the latest iOS 26.3+ or the Background Security Improvements ❨BSI❩ update. → Enable Lockdown Mode for high-risk users to reduce the attack surface for WebKit-based exploits. → Use MDM to isolate any devices that are no longer receiving critical security patches.
#Cybersecurity #MobileSecurity #iOS #Spyware #ThreatIntelligence #CodeDefence

item.title: CISA orders federal agencies to patch critical SharePoint RCE flaw today. 🛑
item.description:
A critical unauthenticated remote code execution flaw in SharePoint is being weaponized in the wild. 🛑
CVE-2026-20963 · Severity 9.8 · CISA KEV Remediation Deadline: March 21‚ 2026.
The @[CISA]\\(urn:li:organization:13010360\\) has added a critical deserialization flaw in @[Microsoft]\\(urn:li:organization:1035\\) SharePoint Server to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. In a network-based attack‚ an unauthenticated attacker can execute arbitrary code to inject and execute commands remotely on the server.
While the flaw was patched in January‚ active exploitation has recently surged. Because SharePoint often stores the core intellectual property and internal communications of an organization‚ an RCE here represents a total compromise of corporate data.
The uncomfortable truth: If your SharePoint servers are internet-exposed and unpatched today‚ you are operating an open repository for automated data exfiltration.
→ Apply the January 2026 security updates for SharePoint Server 2016‚ 2019‚ and Subscription Edition immediately. → Conduct a retrospective audit of your SharePoint logs for unauthorized code injection or anomalous service account activity. → Ensure that internal SharePoint instances are not reachable from the public internet without a Zero Trust gateway.
#Cybersecurity #DataProtection #SharePoint #PatchManagement #SOC #CodeDefence

item.title: Critical Zimbra XSS flaw added to CISA KEV following active attacks. 🐚
item.description:
Your corporate email is being targeted through malicious CSS @import directives. 🐚
CVE-2025-66376 · Severity 7.2 · Active Stored XSS in Zimbra Collaboration Suite.
The @[CISA]\\(urn:li:organization:13010360\\) has added a stored cross-site scripting ❨XSS❩ vulnerability in the Zimbra Classic UI to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Attackers are abusing CSS @import directives in HTML emails to execute malicious scripts in the context of the user session.
This allows threat actors to steal session cookies‚ hijack email accounts‚ and exfiltrate sensitive communications. This vulnerability has been observed in targeted campaigns against government agencies and research institutions throughout early 2026.
The uncomfortable truth: A single malicious email can compromise your entire executive communication history if your mail platform fails to neutralize complex styling directives.
→ Update Synacor Zimbra Collaboration Suite to version 9.0.0 P41‚ 10.0.9‚ or 10.1.1 immediately. → Instruct users to utilize the Modern UI where possible‚ as the vulnerability primarily impacts the Classic UI. → Monitor mail server logs for anomalous outbound traffic originating from webmail user sessions.
#Cybersecurity #EmailSecurity #Zimbra #VulnerabilityManagement #SOC #CodeDefence
